messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Generative AI for Statistical Science (Free Seminar) – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Generative AI for Statistical Science (Free Seminar) – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance to join our 1-day seminar Generative AI for Statistical Science (Free Seminar) livestreaming March 3 with Lei Ding (Department of Statistics, University of Manitoba). This seminar introduces modern generative AI paradigms, covering models like VAEs, GANs, and diffusion models, which are powerful tools for approximating high-dimensional probability distributions. Researchers in genetic data analysis can leverage these methods for tasks such as generating synthetic genetic datasets for privacy-preserving research or for advanced imputation of missing genetic markers. Understanding these statistical foundations will enable the R-sig-genetics community to innovate in modeling complex genetic architectures and address critical issues like data privacy and bias in their studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/generative-ai-for-statistical-science-fr) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Useful Tools for Data Science in R – online seminar
messages.subject_clean: Useful Tools for Data Science in R – online seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Useful Tools for Data Science in R led by SMCS (Universit? Catholique de Louvain) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This seminar explores powerful tools like R, GitHub, Copilot, and Visual Studio Code to enhance data science workflows. Researchers analyzing genetic data can leverage these skills to streamline their R scripting, manage complex analysis pipelines with robust version control, and collaborate more effectively on large-scale genomic projects. Integrating AI-powered coding assistance and efficient development environments will accelerate the development of novel genetic models and ensure the reproducibility and integrity of their research findings.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/useful-tools-for-data-science-in-r-1) at your own pace and please share this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Advanced R Spatial Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Discover advanced methods for analyzing spatial data effectively in your work. Advanced R for Spatial Analysis led by Nick Bearman (University College London and Geospatial Training Solutions) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This seminar focuses on advanced spatial analysis techniques in R, including spatial joins, buffering, and point-in-polygon operations. Researchers in genetics can apply these methods to analyze geographically distributed genetic data, understand population structures in relation to environmental factors, or map disease prevalence. Mastering these skills will enable them to integrate diverse spatial datasets, enhancing the precision and scope of their genetic research and contributing to more robust findings.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/advanced-r-for-spatial-analysis-1) whenever convenient and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last Chance: AI for Systematic Reviews
messages.subject_clean: Last Chance: AI for Systematic Reviews
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - This seminar offers practical, hands-on training to enhance your research workflow and evidence synthesis capabilities. AI for Systematic Reviews is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 11?12 with Hadi Fariborzi (Bissett School of Business, Mount Royal University, Calgary). This seminar introduces a rigorous AI-integrated workflow for systematic reviews, demonstrating how to manage the accelerating volume of genetic literature efficiently. Researchers in genetic data analysis can apply these methods to build reproducible searches, conduct AI-assisted screening, and create analysis-ready datasets for their own meta-analyses. The course emphasizes quantitative workflows, including advanced meta-analysis techniques demonstrated in R, which can directly enhance the rigor and scope of genetic research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-for-systematic-reviews) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Marine Genomics course - online, 25-29 May
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are excited to announce that our Marine Genomics course, originally planned in-person, will now be fully online to foster international participation!
 
Over five days (25?29 May), this course offers hands-on training in:

Genome assembly assessment and variant calling

Population genomics analyses with marine datasets

Integrating genomic and environmental data for seascape genomics

Interpreting results for ecology, conservation, and fisheries
The course combines lectures, discussions, and practical exercises using real marine datasets. It is designed for graduate students, postdocs, and researchers working in marine and population genomics. 
 
You can find more information about the course here: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/marine-genomics/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/marine-genomics/ )
 
 
 
Best regards, Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Evidence Synthesis and Meta-analysis in R | Online Course, 11–14 May
messages.subject_clean: Evidence Synthesis and Meta-analysis in R | Online Course, 11–14 May
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

 
You can still join our 4-day online course ?Meta-analysis in R? (11?14 May) to learn how to conduct rigorous and reproducible evidence synthesis in ecology and evolution.
 
In this hands-on course, participants will learn how to design and perform systematic reviews and meta-analyses, from question formulation and literature searching to effect size calculation, meta-analytic modelling, and interpretation of results. The course also covers multilevel meta-analysis, meta-regression, heterogeneity, publication bias, and data visualization, using R and widely used packages such as metafor and orchaRd.
 
The program combines lectures and practical exercises with real datasets, providing participants with the skills needed to apply evidence synthesis methods to their own research projects.
 
If you are interested in learning how to integrate findings across studies and perform robust meta-analyses in R, this course will provide the practical tools to get started: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ) 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org
